title: Inositol Caps UK — Review Analysis
subtitle: TBD · Amazon UK marketplace · 363 reviews
cpSummary: Customer profile based on 363 reviews scraped from amazon.co.uk's product page. The brand (QSTA / Rola Brand) is Spanish-led, so Amazon EU's cross-marketplace pooling pulls heavy ES/IT/FR reviews alongside English ones — the file is genuinely multilingual (≈29% ES, 23% IT, 13% en-UK, 11% FR, plus a long mixed tail). The most common buyer is a woman aged 25–45 with PCOS using the supplement daily for 1–6 months to regulate her cycle, reduce PCOS symptoms (acne, hair loss, irregular periods), support fertility / trying-to-conceive, or manage insulin resistance. Negatives concentrate around a recent formula change (D-chiro inositol dropped from 100mg to 40mg, folate swapped for folic acid), 6 large capsules per day to hit the advertised dose, and side effects (acne, GI upset, headaches) that no other PCOS supplement seems to surface as visibly.

usageScenarios.label: PCOS daily support (cycle + symptoms)
usageScenarios.reason: primary use case — diagnosed PCOS women take it daily for 1–6+ months hoping to regulate cycles, reduce acne, manage hair loss, and improve hormonal balance.

usageScenarios.label: Cycle regulation after the pill / amenorrhea
usageScenarios.reason: women coming off oral contraceptives or with missing/irregular periods take it specifically to restore a regular cycle.

usageScenarios.label: Trying to conceive (TTC) / fertility support
usageScenarios.reason: couples actively trying to conceive use it to improve ovulation, egg quality and cycle predictability — often paired with prenatal vitamins.

usageScenarios.label: Insulin resistance / blood-sugar management
usageScenarios.reason: women diagnosed with insulin resistance, pre-diabetes or persistent cravings take it to improve glycaemic control alongside diet.

usageScenarios.label: Hormonal acne / skin-clearing protocol
usageScenarios.reason: users with chin/jawline cyclical acne take it as a hormonal-driver fix after topicals and contraceptive pills failed.

usageScenarios.label: Cheaper alternative to pharmacy inositol
usageScenarios.reason: buyers explicitly compare to Ovusitol, Inofolic, Chirofert — looking for the same actives at a lower price on Amazon.

usageScenarios.label: PMS / premenstrual symptom relief
usageScenarios.reason: users with painful periods, breast tenderness, or heavy PMS take it to soften premenstrual symptoms.

usageScenarios.label: Energy / mood / sleep support
usageScenarios.reason: secondary motivation — users report reduced fatigue, calmer mood, deeper sleep within a few weeks.

usageScenarios.label: Hair loss / hirsutism management
usageScenarios.reason: PCOS-driven hair thinning and unwanted facial hair — users take it expecting the inositol + chromium combination to slow it.

csSummary: Sentiment is positive overall (63% positive, 26% negative, 11% neutral). The dominant positive story is cycle regulation and PCOS symptom relief over 1–3 months, with strong cross-language consistency. Negatives cluster around three distinct concerns: (1) a recent formula change — D-chiro inositol cut from 100mg to 40mg and methylfolate replaced with folic acid — broke trust with informed buyers who specifically researched the 40:1 ratio; (2) 6 large capsules a day to hit the advertised 4100mg dose, which the listing initially underplays; (3) side effects (acne flare-ups, GI upset, headaches, fatigue) that customers feel are insufficiently warned.

negativeTopics.label: Formula changed (D-chiro dropped, folic acid swap)
negativeTopics.reason: the most strategic negative — informed PCOS buyers specifically chose this product for its 40:1 myo:D-chiro ratio (100mg D-chiro) and methylfolate. The new formulation drops D-chiro to 40mg and substitutes folic acid for methylfolate, which is unusable for the ~10% with MTHFR polymorphism.
bullets: Customers compare old vs new bottles side-by-side and flag specific mg drops
Particularly painful for the MTHFR-aware crowd — folic acid causes symptoms for them
Listing wasn't updated when formula changed — feels deceptive, not just disappointing
Several repeat buyers cancelling subscription / returning to pharmacy brand
quotes: "Before it has 100mg d chiro inositol but in new formula just 40mg."
"The formula has changed — it's been twice that I'm receiving a version with folic acid instead of folate. I can't have it, I have MTHFR polymorphism."
"Bought for the 40:1 myo:D-chiro ratio but it's been modified — D-chiro is now less than half. Not the product I ordered."

negativeTopics.label: No effect after 1–6 months
negativeTopics.reason: customers complete a full bottle (or several) without seeing cycle regulation, fewer PCOS symptoms, or improved fertility — feel money was wasted.
bullets: Often the customer has tried other inositol brands successfully before — direct comparison
Many had stable cycles previously, started this product, and regressed
Several abandoned after 3+ months and returned to pharmacy product (Ovusitol, Inofolic)
quotes: "After one month of using inositol, I have to say it is a no for me. I have PCOS and wanted to avoid paying the extortionate amount of the pharmacy version — but I'm afraid I have to go back to it."
"I've been on it 4 months — first month bright, then my period was delayed 54 days. I'm going back to my doctor."
"3 months of cure, no improvement at all. A waste of money."

negativeTopics.label: Caused / worsened acne
negativeTopics.reason: a non-trivial subset of users report new severe acne, especially after 4–6 weeks. Several cite Reddit threads with similar reports. Particularly damaging because reducing acne is itself a top purchase motivation.
bullets: Acne typically appears at week 4–6 of use, clears within 2–3 weeks of stopping
Some users report it alongside other hormonal symptoms (sleep disturbance, painful periods)
Mentioned often by buyers who came specifically for hormonal acne — double disappointment
Reddit cross-referenced by multiple reviewers — risks public reputational damage
quotes: "Took this for around 5–6 weeks. I was left with severe acne — before I had the occasional spot. According to Reddit a lot of people experienced the same thing. Never again."
"After the second month I had to stop because acne appeared."
"My periods are more painful, my acne got much worse, I sleep worse. Unsafe product."

negativeTopics.label: Side effects — nausea, cramps, headaches
negativeTopics.reason: GI upset, severe headaches, fatigue, hot flushes, breathlessness reported especially in the first 1–2 weeks. Some customers stop after a few days; others push through.
bullets: Headaches and migraines are the most repeated single side effect
Cramps, nausea and stomach pain often forced cessation within 10 days
A few customers report severe symptoms — breathlessness, vomiting, vertigo — and unwell for days
No prominent warning on the packaging that side effects are common
quotes: "I had to stop after a dozen days because I had heavy side effects: headaches, nausea, vertigo, intense fatigue."
"After just a week: difficulty breathing, nausea/vomiting, stomach cramps, hot flushes. Horrible side effects."
"Headaches, nausea, bloating — disappointed the side effects weren't mentioned anywhere. Wasted €30+."

negativeTopics.label: 6 capsules to hit advertised dose — misleading
negativeTopics.reason: the main image shouts '4100mg / 9-in-1' but customers discover you must take 6 large capsules per day to actually receive that amount — listing implies 3 capsules suffice. Bottle becomes a 1-month supply, not 2.
bullets: Customers feel mis-sold on per-capsule potency vs per-daily-dose
Capsules are described as 'huge', 'enormous', 'ludicrous' to take 6 of
Effective monthly cost is double what buyers expected
Some report difficulty swallowing 6 large capsules / day
quotes: "6 capsules to make for 4000mg daily dosage is ludicrous. Misleading info — leading you to believe you only need 3 capsules a day until you receive the box."
"You have to take 3 or 6 capsules a day to reach the normal dose — that's not stated in the listing. Plus the ratio 40:1 isn't respected."
"The dose seems high but it's actually 6 capsules. Effectively low per-capsule dose for the size of the pills."

negativeTopics.label: Made it worse / hormonal disruption
negativeTopics.reason: more alarming than 'no effect' — customers report cycles becoming more irregular, prolonged bleeding, spotting, weight gain, or worsened PCOS symptoms after starting. Symptoms typically resolve after stopping.
bullets: Prolonged bleeding (multiple weeks) and emergency room visits in the worst cases
Weight gain despite controlled diet, reversing within 2 weeks of stopping
Hirsutism (facial hair) worsened in non-insulin-resistant women — biology-aware reviewers explicitly flag D-chiro for this segment
Trust collapse — customer permanently switches to a competing product
quotes: "After 3 weeks I had period pain and spotting, then my actual period came a week late. The negative effects were completely absent of any benefit."
"Spent a year of money for nothing — it actually made my situation worse. Now I take Chirofert and my cycle is regular. Endometrium thickening that needed a gynae visit. I learned the hard way."
"I have PCOS — but not insulin-resistant. The D-chiro at this dose made my hirsutism worse. Be aware that without IR, high D-chiro can backfire."

negativeTopics.label: No origin transparency / suspicious brand
negativeTopics.reason: customers cannot identify the manufacturing laboratory; the label says 'distributed by Rola Brand Barcelona' and points to a Miami WhatsApp number. Reviewers describe the brand as 'an influencer product' rather than a clinical supplement, which triggers safety concerns.
bullets: Label gives only a distributor in Barcelona, no manufacturing site or lab
WhatsApp support number is US-based (Miami) — odd for an EU supplement
Multiple reviewers describe it as an 'influencer product' without scientific backing
Buyers express discomfort consuming a supplement of unknown origin
quotes: "I contacted the seller via WhatsApp on the label — a Miami Florida number. I asked where the product is manufactured: the label only says distributed by Rola Brand Barcelona, no manufacturer or lab listed."
"Bought based on reviews, but the listing gives zero info about the laboratory. It seems made by an influencer with lots of followers but very little interaction — suspicious."
"I returned it because it's impossible to know where it's produced — and for an ingestible product that worries me a lot."

negativeTopics.label: Pharmacy alternative is better
negativeTopics.reason: informed buyers (often with MTHFR, insulin resistance, or fertility goals) explicitly compare to Ovusitol / Inofolic / Chirofert — and say the pharmacy product wins on results, formulation purity, or trust.
bullets: Several customers returned to pharmacy version after this product failed
Pharmacy products are 2–3× more expensive but customers accept the premium
Drives a specific 'switching back' segment — a lost-customer pattern
Reinforces the perception that this is a cut-price imitation, not a genuine clinical formula
quotes: "I wanted to avoid paying the extortionate amount of the pharmacy version — but I'm afraid I have to go back to it. PCOS symptoms not diminished."
"This is not an equivalent for the pharmacy product. Going back to my pharmacy brand."
"Next time I'll spend more and go to the pharmacy. Beyond the QR-code chat there's no real information here."

negativeTopics.label: Review-for-freebie scheme erodes trust
negativeTopics.reason: the bottle includes a QR code that, when scanned, offers a 2-for-1 in exchange for a 5-star review. Multiple reviewers explicitly call this out and warn future buyers that the rating average is unreliable.
bullets: Brand offers a free second bottle for a 5-star review (scanned QR confirmation)
Negative reviewers explicitly name this practice in their 1-star reviews
Pollutes Amazon rating signal — review skeptics warn each other in the negatives
Risk of Amazon enforcement / vine penalty if reported
quotes: "They offer a 2x1 if you leave a review. I gave 5 stars and got a free bottle — that's why this product has so many 5-star reviews. It's a scam."
"Right after I bought it, on the package there was a QR code saying 'scan for a free bottle'. The positive reviews are bought."
"The reviews are paid for. Customer service asks for a positive review in exchange for a discount on your next purchase."

negativeTopics.label: Packaging defects / quality concerns
negativeTopics.reason: broken safety seals, powder residue inside the bottle, capsules damaged open, packaging not matching the listing photo. Triggers safety doubts on an ingestible supplement.
bullets: Powder residue inside the bottle on arrival reported by several buyers
Some capsules arrived open/damaged with contents spilled in the bottle
Packaging design not matching the listing photo — customers question authenticity
Triggers immediate return without trying — direct revenue loss
quotes: "When I received the package there was powder residue all over the inside of the bottle — the contents did not seem safe to consume. Item was returned."
"Some capsules arrived open, powder spilled inside the bottle. Disappointing."
"The packaging is different from the listing photo. Doesn't match."

negativeInsights.finding: The recent formula change (D-chiro 100mg → 40mg, methylfolate → folic acid) is independently flagged in dozens of negatives across DE/ES/IT/FR/EN. The listing image wasn't updated, so informed PCOS buyers feel deceived.
negativeInsights.implication: Either restore the original formula or update the listing transparently with a 'Reformulated' badge + explanation of why. Hiding the change is poisoning the loyal-buyer segment that brought this product to scale.

negativeInsights.finding: Side effects (acne flare-ups, severe headaches, GI upset, prolonged bleeding) are reported well above what competitor pharmacy products surface. Customers explicitly say 'no warning was provided'.
negativeInsights.implication: Add explicit warnings on label + listing: 'Some women without insulin resistance may experience worsening hirsutism with D-chiro inositol at this dose. Consult your doctor if you have MTHFR or are TTC.' Reduces the safety-flag reviews driving 1-star ratings.

negativeInsights.finding: The main image leads with '4100mg / 9-in-1' but the daily dose requires 6 large capsules — buyers feel the per-capsule potency was hidden. This drives a wave of '6 enormous capsules!' 1-star reviews on otherwise indifferent customers.
negativeInsights.implication: Disclose '6 capsules/day' prominently in image 2 and bullet 1. Position the high-capsule-count as 'split-dose, gentle' rather than hide it. Adds expectation honesty without losing the headline dose claim.

negativeInsights.finding: Customers can't identify the manufacturing lab — only 'distributed by Rola Brand Barcelona' + a Miami WhatsApp number. Multiple reviewers describe it as 'an influencer product'. This is structurally different from competitor pharmacy brands.
negativeInsights.implication: Add manufacturing site + GMP/ISO certification badges to the listing and label. Without that, the formula-change negatives compound into a 'this is sketchy' brand narrative.

negativeInsights.finding: Review-for-freebie scheme (QR code → 2x1 for 5-star review) is independently named by 4–5% of negative reviewers. Amazon takedown risk + rating signal corruption.
negativeInsights.implication: Stop the practice immediately. Acceptable Vine programme uplift is one thing; explicit 'free bottle for 5-star' is another. Amazon's policy team penalises this aggressively when surfaced.

positiveTopics.label: Regulated my cycle / brought my period back
positiveTopics.reason: the single most repeated positive — women with irregular or absent periods (often PCOS or post-pill) report a regular cycle within 1–3 months of consistent use.
bullets: Effect typically reported after 1 full bottle (1–2 months)
Cycle length normalises to 28–33 days in many cases
Particularly powerful for women who'd been on hormonal contraception for years and feared their natural cycle was lost
Strong cross-language consistency (ES/IT/FR/EN)
quotes: "Years on contraceptives, no doctor thought I'd have regular periods again. Since taking this: regular periods, first three months 33 days, now 28. I'm happy!"
"After 10 years on the pill due to PCOS, never had stable periods. 30 days in and I had my first period, and another exactly 28 days later. I'm 40 and finally regular."
"Life changing! I'm finally getting regular periods and my hormones feel balanced after 2 months on this."

positiveTopics.label: PCOS symptom improvement (broad)
positiveTopics.reason: diagnosed PCOS users report aggregate improvement — fewer cysts, less hair thinning, less acne, more energy, more regular ovulation. The product earns long-term loyalty in this segment.
bullets: Improvement typically across multiple PCOS axes at once
Most satisfaction in users 2–6 months in, not first-month buyers
Several users report a successful pregnancy after months of PCOS-related infertility
Ovulation tracking strips used by some to confirm — credible proof signal
quotes: "I have diagnosed PCOS. 3 months on these — no more acne breakouts, and ovulation strips confirm I'm ovulating again. 100% recommend."
"2 years trying to regulate my PCOS, tried tons of supplements and ovarian stimulation without success. 3 months on this and I ovulated naturally for the first time in 2 years!"
"After years of doctors telling me it was impossible — I even had an IUD inside — I got pregnant after starting inositol."

positiveTopics.label: Customer service + QR-code support
positiveTopics.reason: post-purchase service via WhatsApp / QR code is consistently praised across all languages — buyers value being told the right personalised dose for their goal (PCOS / fertility / acne).
bullets: QR code on bottle directs to product info + personalised dosing chat
Responsive WhatsApp / chat support praised even by some critical reviewers
Personalised guidance (which goal → which dose) appreciated by first-time buyers
True cross-market differentiator — competitor supplements rarely match this
quotes: "Customer service is perfect — they're extremely helpful and explain how to use the product perfectly. The QR code lets you get in touch with professionals."
"There's a QR code where you scan and they recommend the best dose for your goal. Easy and very useful — that alone justifies part of the price."
"Service client réactif et très compréhensif — 5/5."

positiveTopics.label: Comprehensive formula / good ingredients
positiveTopics.reason: informed buyers (especially in English-language Vine-style reviews) praise the 9-in-1 stack: myo + D-chiro inositol, methylfolate, B6, B12, D3, chromium, zinc, manganese, selenium. Feels complete vs single-ingredient competitors.
bullets: Multi-ingredient stack saves buying separate B-complex and trace minerals
Vegan, gluten-free, soy-free, dairy-free framing well received
180-capsule pack feels generous when first compared to pharmacy brands
Chromium picolinate addition specifically valued for blood-sugar / cravings
quotes: "The only product I've found that has all the ingredients and the correct ratio of inositol needed to support women with PCOS — and at a great price."
"9-in-1 formulation includes inositol, folate 5-MTHF, chromium, zinc, B6, B12, D3 — crucial for hormonal and reproductive health."
"Comprehensive blend designed for female health without compromising on quality. Vegan, gluten/soy/dairy-free, no artificial fillers."

positiveTopics.label: Reduced acne / better skin & hair
positiveTopics.reason: users with hormonal/cyclical acne and PCOS-driven hair thinning report visible skin clearing and hair regrowth after 1–3 months. The opposite signal also exists (see negatives) — outcome bifurcates by user profile.
bullets: Acne improvement typically reported alongside cycle regulation
Hair density / less shedding mentioned by PCOS-with-hair-loss users
Glycaemic-spike-driven skin reactivity reduced (less post-meal puffiness)
Effect builds gradually — month 2–3 is when users notice
quotes: "Bought it for my hormonal chin acne that flared with every period — after 3 months no more breakouts and ovulation strips confirm I'm cycling again."
"It's made my hair less thin and I feel like I have more energy."
"Helping me resolve my skin issues — at night I take it and morning glycaemic spikes are smaller, fewer skin reactions."

positiveTopics.label: Easy to swallow / tasteless / no aftertaste
positiveTopics.reason: capsules described as standard size, neutral taste, no smell — daily-adherence factor that supports continued use. Some users praise gentle GI tolerance.
bullets: Vegan capsule shell — easy to swallow with water
No taste / no smell / no aftertaste — frictionless daily compliance
Some users open the capsule and drink in water for capsule-averse buyers
No reflux reported by most positive reviewers
quotes: "Capsules are very easy to swallow and have no flavor — very convenient to use."
"Easy to swallow, no aftertaste, easy to take. Recommended."
"No nasty smells or tastes, and the supplements are easy to swallow."

positiveTopics.label: Good value vs pharmacy alternatives
positiveTopics.reason: users who compared to pharmacy products (Ovusitol, Inofolic, Inositolfem) repeatedly mention this product is significantly cheaper for comparable formulation — a major purchase driver.
bullets: 180-capsule pack at ~€20–25 vs pharmacy at €40–50 for similar dose
Specifically cited as 'the affordable alternative' for chronic PCOS use
Repeat-purchase positives often mention this is now their long-term brand
Subscribe & Save behaviour evident in some review threads
quotes: "Compared to other PCOS supplements I'm seeing this as all-in-one and more affordable."
"Good quality and decent value for money — I'll be ordering it again."
"Excellent quality-to-price ratio for a supplement — clearly more affordable than pharmacy alternatives."

positiveTopics.label: Less PMS / less pain / better mood + sleep
positiveTopics.reason: secondary benefits — reduced PMS symptoms, less period pain, calmer mood, deeper sleep. Often emerging in month 2–3 of use.
bullets: Breast tenderness and pre-menstrual spotting reduced
Period pain noticeably lower in repeat-cycle users
Better sleep + less anxiety reported by ~5% of positives
Energy improvement vs previous chronic fatigue
quotes: "This has really helped me with my PMS symptoms — I feel less irritable and the pain isn't as bad."
"PMS-wise, really top, I don't have it anymore — got back almost all my energy."
"Migraine reduced from almost daily to only cycle days, pre-menstrual spotting reduced from 5–7 days to 1–2."

positiveInsights.finding: Cycle regulation + PCOS symptom improvement together account for ~47% of all positive comments — by far the biggest tangible outcome customers attribute to the product.
positiveInsights.implication: Make 'Regulates your cycle within 1–3 months' the primary headline. PCOS support sits underneath. Other claims (fertility, blood sugar, acne) become supporting bullets, not co-headliners.

positiveInsights.finding: QR-code + WhatsApp personalised dosing support is mentioned positively across all four languages — unique vs pharmacy and Amazon competitors, who ship a bottle and disappear.
positiveInsights.implication: Lead the listing image 2 / A+ section with 'Free personalised dosing support — scan QR, tell us your goal, get a tailored plan.' Easy to verify, almost impossible for competitors to copy at scale.

positiveInsights.finding: Highest satisfaction is among PCOS-diagnosed women using it as a 2–6+ month maintenance product — not first-time short-term buyers. Several explicitly report successful pregnancy after months of use.
positiveInsights.implication: Subscribe & Save program targeted at the long-term PCOS segment. Build a 90-day 'PCOS protocol' content piece (A+, email follow-up) — this segment churns less and is the sustainable revenue base.

positiveInsights.finding: Value-vs-pharmacy framing is a top-3 purchase driver. Users explicitly name Ovusitol, Inofolic, Chirofert and choose this product for the price gap.
positiveInsights.implication: Add a price-comparison A+ module: 'Same actives at a fraction of pharmacy cost — without compromising on 40:1 ratio or methylfolate.' Critical that the formula matches the claim — see the formula-change risk in negatives.

buyersMotivation.label: Regulate cycle / restore period
buyersMotivation.reason: primary trigger — irregular cycles, absent periods, post-pill amenorrhea. Often the explicit reason cited in the title of the review.

buyersMotivation.label: Manage PCOS symptoms (broad)
buyersMotivation.reason: diagnosed PCOS women looking for a single supplement covering cycle + hormones + acne + hair.

buyersMotivation.label: Improve fertility / TTC
buyersMotivation.reason: couples trying to conceive, often after months/years of difficulty, sometimes with PCOS or low ovarian reserve.

buyersMotivation.label: Cheaper alternative to pharmacy inositol
buyersMotivation.reason: informed users specifically benchmarking against Ovusitol, Inofolic, Chirofert.

buyersMotivation.label: Reduce hormonal acne
buyersMotivation.reason: users with cyclical chin/jawline acne who've exhausted topicals and contraceptive pills.

buyersMotivation.label: Improve insulin sensitivity / blood sugar
buyersMotivation.reason: diagnosed insulin resistance, pre-diabetes, or persistent cravings + post-meal energy crashes.

buyersMotivation.label: Better energy / mood / sleep
buyersMotivation.reason: secondary motivation — users hoping for a general vitality / hormonal-balance lift.

buyersMotivation.label: Recommended by doctor / gynaecologist
buyersMotivation.reason: explicit medical recommendation; user looking for a high-dose, well-rated Amazon option to fulfil the recommendation.

customerExpectations.label: Formula as advertised (40:1 ratio, methylfolate not folic acid)
customerExpectations.reason: informed PCOS buyers chose this for the specific clinical formulation. Discovering a silently reformulated product is the single biggest expectation gap.

customerExpectations.label: Visible cycle / PCOS improvement within 1–3 months
customerExpectations.reason: buyers expect to see regularity or symptom relief by end of first or second bottle. Slower responders or non-responders feel mis-sold.

customerExpectations.label: Honest per-capsule potency disclosure
customerExpectations.reason: expected '4100mg per serving' to mean per capsule or per 3 capsules. Discovered they need 6 large capsules/day — daily cost effectively doubled.

customerExpectations.label: No / minimal side effects
customerExpectations.reason: expected a gentle, well-tolerated supplement. Got headaches, nausea, acne flare-ups, fatigue, bleeding irregularities — with no label warning.

customerExpectations.label: Verifiable manufacturing origin
customerExpectations.reason: expected a clear manufacturer + lab / GMP certification. Got a 'distributed by' line and a Miami WhatsApp number — concerning for an ingestible.

customerExpectations.label: Authentic reviews (no incentivised 5-star scheme)
customerExpectations.reason: customers chose the product because of a high average rating, then discovered the freebie-for-review practice — feel deceived.

customerExpectations.label: Sealed, undamaged packaging
customerExpectations.reason: expected an unopened, full, sealed bottle. Repeated reports of powder residue, damaged capsules, mismatched packaging undermine basic trust.

customerExpectations.label: Personalised dosing guidance
customerExpectations.reason: expected to know how to take the product for their specific goal (PCOS / fertility / acne). The QR-code chat meets this expectation when delivery is fast.
